The film has headed into Day 5 today since its release on June 19, 2026. With a reported production budget of Rs 150 crore, the film's weekday hold has become critical to reaching break-even and the clock is ticking, with Akshay Kumar's 'Welcome to the Jungle' set to arrive in cinemas this Friday alongside a resurgent 'Main Vaapas Aaunga.

description: Veteran actor Mammootty, known for his grounded perspective, shared profound thoughts on fame and remembrance in a recent interview. He candidly stated, "Don’t expect people to remember you until the end of the world," emphasizing the transient nature of public memory. Mammootty, who has no plans for retirement, believes that even great individuals are remembered for a limited time, urging a focus on present value rather than eternal recognition.

Bhu-Aadhaar for land: How a 14-digit ID can help property buyers to avoid disputes
description: India is introducing Bhu-Aadhaar, a 14-digit Unique Land Parcel Identification Number (ULPIN), to digitally identify every land parcel. This initiative aims to simplify property verification by creating a standard digital record, making transactions easier for buyers, banks, and government agencies. Based on geo-referenced coordinates, ULPIN ensures a unique digital identity tied to each plot's exact location, strengthening land administration and potentially reducing disputes.

description: US secretary of state Marco Rubio is heading to the Gulf amid controversy over a proposed $300 billion reconstruction fund for Iran. While critics decry it as a US payout, the administration claims Gulf states and private investors would largely finance it. However, some Arab allies fear a wealthier Iran could boost its regional influence and military might, sparking unease despite suggestions of significant foreign interest in investing.

title: 'Starstruck' by Sooryavanshi: Broad quips 'he's even getting into sledging now'
description: Former England pacer Stuart Broad expressed immense excitement to witness 15-year-old Indian batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi during the upcoming UK T20I series, admitting he might be starstruck. Sooryavanshi's record-breaking List-A fifty has propelled him into India's senior squad, drawing praise from Broad and Jos Buttler for his explosive talent and rapid rise, making him a significant challenge for England.

title: Iran says US agreed to release $12 billion in frozen assets; Washington yet to confirm
Ayodhya donation scam probe: SIT submits preliminary report to UP govt
description: A Special Investigation Team has submitted its initial findings to the Uttar Pradesh government regarding allegations of embezzlement of donations for the Ayodhya Ram temple. The probe, initiated by the temple trust, is actively continuing as investigators gather more information. Officials confirmed the preliminary report was handed over to the Additional Chief Secretary (Home) by the Lucknow Divisional Commissioner, with further details expected as the investigation progresses.

title: El Nino woes: Why weak monsoon may turn out to be a bigger worry for India than US-Iran war
description: A normal monsoon is important for several reasons - most of all for the way it feeds into the economy’s growth and inflationary routes. A below average monsoon impacts crop sowing and harvest, which in turn leads to prices of vegetables and staple food rising.

title: 'India welcomes US-Iran MoU': NSA Ajit Doval expresses 'cautious optimism' over peace deal
description: India's National Security Advisor Ajit Doval expressed 'cautious optimism' regarding the US-Iran MoU, hoping it restores regional stability and strengthens energy security. He highlighted the potential for easing global supply chain disruptions and improving economic prosperity through the Strait of Hormuz. Doval also addressed the world's complex security landscape and the weakening of multilateral institutions, emphasizing BRICS' role in fostering a multipolar order and cooperation among emerging economies.

After Pakistan, now Bangladesh eyes China’s J-10CE fighter: Why it matters
description: Bangladesh is reportedly set to acquire 24 Chinese J-10CE fighter jets, a significant move for both nations. This deal, if finalized, would mark the second export for China's advanced fighter, following Pakistan's acquisition. The procurement aims to bolster the Bangladeshi Air Force's combat capabilities, replacing aging F-7 aircraft. This development occurs as China's defense exports face a downturn, with Asia being a primary market.

description: A horrific incident has gripped Delhi as an 11-year-old girl was allegedly kidnapped, brutally assaulted, and murdered after being taken from a South Delhi footpath. Police swiftly arrested a suspect following a rigorous investigation involving CCTV analysis and technical evidence. The victim's body was discovered in a forested area near the Delhi-Gurugram border. Authorities are continuing their interrogation to uncover any potential accomplices.

title: 'France will probably beat us': Haaland's reality check ahead of Mbappe showdown
description: The Manchester City forward now has four goals in the World Cup, level with France's Kylian Mbappe and one behind Lionel Messi. Norway's victory also takes them to six points in Group I, alongside France, with the two sides set to meet on Friday to decide the group winner.

Amid rebellion, TMC sends fresh office-bearers list to EC, says Mamata remains party chief
description: Trinamool Congress has reshuffled its national working committee, asserting Mamata Banerjee's leadership following a significant electoral setback. This move aims to counter a rebellion by a faction claiming to be the "real" TMC, which appointed its own chairperson. The party has informed the Election Commission of its new office-bearers, with Banerjee remaining chairperson and her nephew Abhishek Banerjee continuing as national general secretary.

description: Tamil Nadu chief minister Vijay engaged in a heated exchange with the opposition DMK in the assembly, accusing them of misusing party funds and engaging in political opportunism. Amidst disruptions, he mimicked a viral hand gesture associated with former CM MK Stalin, leading to a DMK walkout. Vijay defended his party's alliances and addressed concerns on law and order, asserting his government's commitment to tackling corruption and crime.

title: 'Bring a diaper, write your demand': CJP's new protest plan at 6pm today
description: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) continues its four-day protest at Jantar Mantar, demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET-UG paper leak. Organizers reported police attempts to restrict protest space overnight. The CJP has announced a "diaper donation drive" and dedicated a day for UPSC and SSC aspirants to voice concerns, vowing to continue their indefinite agitation.

Assam HSLC Compartment Result 2026 declared at sebaonline.org: Direct link, steps to download marksheet
description: The Assam State School Education Board (ASSEB) has declared the HSLC Compartment Result 2026 on June 23 at 11 AM. Students who appeared for the supplementary examination can access and download their scorecards from the official website, sebaonline.org, using their roll number and login credentials. Candidates who qualify will be eligible for Class 11 admissions and other academic opportunities. The board has also advised students to verify details on their marksheets and follow official updates.

description: US President Donald Trump has sharply criticized outgoing UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's policies on energy, immigration, and foreign relations, suggesting they contributed to his downfall. Trump specifically pointed to Starmer's approach to North Sea oil and the UK's energy independence. He also mentioned a disagreement over the use of a British military base in Cyprus for US strikes on Iran, which he believes "hurt him badly."

India taps US for cooking gas: LPG imports to cross 1 M tonnes amid Middle East disruption
description: India's LPG imports from the US are set to hit a record 1 million tonnes in June, a significant shift driven by Middle Eastern supply disruptions. Despite higher costs, New Delhi is prioritizing uninterrupted cooking fuel for households. While traditional suppliers are gradually recovering, the US has become a crucial alternative source, highlighting a strategic pivot in energy sourcing.

title: SEBI's new ease-of-business push: What may change for stock exchanges, brokers
description: SEBI is embarking on a substantial reform of stock market regulations designed to simplify operational procedures and alleviate compliance burdens. This review focuses on consolidating rules, minimizing redundancies, and reallocating responsibilities among market infrastructure institutions. Major proposals encompass the creation of a unified master circular for exchanges, updated technology frameworks, and refined reporting processes, ultimately aiming to improve the ease of doing business for all market participants.

'Goal of overtaking the world': Kim calls for major expansion of North Korea's nukes
description: North Korea's Kim Jong-un has declared a significant expansion of the nation's nuclear arsenal, aiming to "overtake the world" in defense capabilities. During a key party meeting, officials reaffirmed their commitment to nuclear forces as crucial for national sovereignty and deterring war. The agenda also included accelerating naval projects, reinforcing border defenses, and addressing economic development, particularly in the coal sector. Leadership changes were also announced without explanation.

Jharkhand high court raises divorced woman's alimony from Rs 10 lakh to Rs 40 lakh
description: The Jharkhand High Court significantly increased a divorced woman's alimony from Rs 10 lakh to Rs 40 lakh. The court recognized her ongoing medical needs and the impact of inflation, ensuring she can maintain a dignified life. This decision acknowledges the husband's financial capacity, including his railway retirement benefits, and upholds the principle of providing support post-divorce.

Despite external criticism, Martínez defended his squad, emphasising unity and dismissing 'unfair' commentary. He highlighted Ronaldo's continued value in creating space and scoring opportunities.

title: FIFA WC: Norway's Viking Row shakes stands after knockout qualification - Watch
description: The Viking Row has become a popular tradition among Norway fans during the tournament. Supporters sit in a formation resembling a Viking longboat and mimic rowing movements to the beat of a drum, drawing comparisons with Iceland's famous "Viking Clap" from Euro 2016.

description: A devastating fire in Aliganj claimed several young lives, leaving families shattered. Among the victims were Abdul Rahman, the sole breadwinner for his paralyzed father and homemaker mother; Sukhmani Singh, who made a desperate plea for help in a final call; Mohammad Shahzan, a beloved only son; and Aditya Srivastava, who pleaded for rescue before succumbing to the blaze. The tragedy has plunged multiple households into profound grief.

The controversial accusations have sparked shockwaves across the league and many are curious about Geno Smith’s alleged ex girlfriend.

description: London's O2 Arena witnessed a dramatic WWE RAW go-home show for Night of Champions.  A beloved star turned heel, shocking fans, while Chad Gable faced his former stablemates. After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
description: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.
